body.title: Audit CC palace rules through approach-frame lens; dissolve do-not rule
body.result: 10 top-level CC palace rules audited: 2 already approach-framed (persona, docs-live-in-code), 7 content-reframed (budget, coding, collaboration, commits, database, igor-constraints, memory), 1 dissolved + reborn (do-not → safeguards, target=clean reversibility + cross-index). CLAUDE.md + MEMORY.md read-order updated as coupling fix. Metric +10 reviewed +8 reframed. Commit 7217cda7.
body.description:
Walk all `theigors/rules/*` palace nodes through the approach-frame lens. For each proscriptive rule (starts with 'no', 'don't', 'never', 'avoid'), propose a positive-target reframe and write it. Specifically: the `do-not` rule is the structural mismatch — it's the prohibition dumping ground. Dissolve it: each entry moves to a positive home in an existing or new rule node.

**Affected files:** palace rows under `theigors/rules/*` (content edits). `theigors/rules/do-not` likely becomes empty and gets archived.

**Design rules:** no-sqlite (clan.memory_palace, Postgres only); approach-frame (this IS the implementation); no-new-memory-schemas (edits to existing rows, no new tables); test-plan-or-why-not — see below.

**Scope boundary:**
- In: reframe existing proscriptive rules to approach-frame. Dissolve do-not node.
- Out: Igor's base rules (T-audit-igor-cp-approach-frame). Out: generated docs (T-sweep-avoidance-frame-docs). Out: feedback_no_* memory files (T-sweep-avoidance-frame-docs).

**Gate:** T-palace-approach-frame-rule (need the canonical rule in place to score against).

**Test plan:** no new tests. Verification: `SELECT path, content FROM memory_palace WHERE path LIKE 'theigors/rules/%'` — no proscriptive openers remain without explicit keep-rationale noted inline. Record sweep count in T-approach-frame-sensor-node metric.
